[DOCUMENT: due_diligence_report_acquico.pdf / Page 1]
HEADER: CONFIDENTIAL — Due Diligence Report: Acquico Technologies Ltd | Prepared by: Deloitte Corporate Finance | Date: November 2024

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
This report presents the findings of financial and commercial due diligence on Acquico Technologies Ltd ('the Target'), conducted on behalf of Vertex Capital Partners ('the Acquirer') in connection with a proposed acquisition at an indicative enterprise value of £85m. The Target is a B2B SaaS business providing workflow automation software to mid-market financial services firms. FY2024 revenue: £9.2m; ARR: £8.7m; Gross Margin: 74%; EBITDA: £0.9m (adjusted).

Key Findings:
• Revenue is highly recurring (94.5% ARR ratio) with strong NRR of 118%
• Customer concentration risk: top 5 customers = 41% of ARR
• Technology stack is modern (cloud-native) but IP ownership clarity needed for 3 components
• 2 ongoing employment tribunal claims (estimated liability: £120,000–£180,000)
• No material tax exposures identified

[DOCUMENT: due_diligence_report_acquico.pdf / Page 2]
HEADER: CONFIDENTIAL — Due Diligence Report: Acquico Technologies Ltd

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE (FY2022–FY2024)

Revenue:
  FY2022: £5.4m | FY2023: £7.1m | FY2024: £9.2m
  CAGR (2-year): 30.5%

Gross Profit:
  FY2022: £3.8m (70.4%) | FY2023: £5.1m (71.8%) | FY2024: £6.8m (73.9%)

EBITDA (Adjusted):
  FY2022: £0.2m | FY2023: £0.5m | FY2024: £0.9m
  Adjustments: £0.4m one-off legal costs (FY2023); £0.3m founder salary normalisation (FY2024)

Cash & Debt (at 30 Sep 2024):
  Cash: £2.1m | Debt: £0.8m (CBILS loan — final repayment Dec 2024) | Net Cash: £1.3m

Working Capital:
  Average debtor days: 28 | Average creditor days: 44 | Net working capital: £0.6m

[DOCUMENT: due_diligence_report_acquico.pdf / Page 3]
HEADER: CONFIDENTIAL — Due Diligence Report: Acquico Technologies Ltd

COMMERCIAL & OPERATIONAL FINDINGS

Customer Analysis:
  Total customers: 84 | New customers (FY2024): 19 | Churned: 3
  NRR: 118% | Gross Retention: 96.4%
  ACV (average): £103,500 | Largest single customer ACV: £820,000

Product & Technology:
  Core product: AutoFlow — cloud-native, AWS-hosted, SOC 2 Type II certified
  Integrations: Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics, Xero, Sage
  R&D spend (FY2024): £1.4m (15.2% of revenue)
  Open source components with unclear licensing: 3 (under legal review)

Headcount:
  Total employees: 67 | Engineering: 28 | Sales & Marketing: 14 | Operations: 12 | G&A: 13
  Key person dependency: CEO (founder) and CTO — retention packages being negotiated

Risks Identified:
  1. Customer concentration — top 5 = 41% ARR
  2. IP ambiguity — 3 open source components
  3. Employment tribunals — est. £120,000–£180,000 liability
  4. Key person risk — CEO and CTO
  5. CBILS loan covenant — expires Dec 2024

Extract all content from all three pages of this due diligence report, covering every section and data point completely.

[DOCUMENT: employee_handbook_excerpt.pdf / Page 1]
HEADER: Orion Financial Group — Employee Handbook | Section 4: Leave Policy | Version 3.2 | Effective 01 January 2025 | HR-POL-004

4.1 ANNUAL LEAVE
Entitlement is based on grade:
  Grade A (Junior):        25 days + 8 public holidays
  Grade B (Mid):           27 days + 8 public holidays
  Grade C (Senior):        30 days + 8 public holidays
  Grade D (Principal/Director): 32 days + 8 public holidays

Leave year runs from 1 January to 31 December. Up to 5 days may be carried forward to the following leave year. Requests must be submitted via the HR portal at least 5 working days in advance for absences of 1–3 days, and at least 10 working days in advance for absences of 4 or more days.

4.2 SICK LEAVE
  During probation (first 6 months):   Statutory Sick Pay (SSP) only
  After 6 months service:              10 days full pay per rolling 12-month period
  After 2 years service:               20 days full pay per rolling 12-month period
  After 5 years service:               30 days full pay per rolling 12-month period

All sick absences of 3 or more consecutive days require a fit note from a GP.

FOOTER: Orion Financial Group Employee Handbook | Section 4 | Page 1 of 2 | HR-POL-004

[DOCUMENT: employee_handbook_excerpt.pdf / Page 2]
HEADER: Orion Financial Group — Employee Handbook | Section 4: Leave Policy | Version 3.2

4.3 FAMILY LEAVE
  Maternity Leave:
    Weeks 1–18:    Full pay
    Weeks 19–26:   Half pay
    Weeks 27–52:   Statutory Maternity Pay (SMP)
    After week 52: Unpaid (up to 52 additional weeks available as Shared Parental Leave)
  Paternity Leave:     2 weeks full pay (must be taken within 8 weeks of birth/adoption)
  Adoption Leave:      Same entitlement as Maternity Leave
  Shared Parental Leave: Up to 50 weeks, paid at SMP rate unless enhanced pay opted into

4.4 OTHER LEAVE TYPES
  Bereavement Leave:
    Immediate family (spouse/partner/child/parent): Up to 10 days paid
    Extended family (grandparent/sibling):          Up to 5 days paid
    Others:                                         Up to 2 days paid at manager discretion
  Jury Service:          Full pay for up to 10 days; statutory payments thereafter
  Study Leave:           Up to 5 days per year for approved qualifications (Grade B and above)
  Volunteering Leave:    2 days per year for approved charitable activities
  Religious Observance:  Up to 3 days per year (taken from annual leave entitlement or unpaid)

FOOTER: Orion Financial Group Employee Handbook | Section 4 | Page 2 of 2 | HR-POL-004

HEADER: THE FINANCIAL CHRONICLE | Tuesday, 14 March 2024 | Price: £2.50 | Established 1892

COLUMN 1:
  MARKETS SURGE ON
  RATE CUT HOPES
  By James Crawford

  Global equity markets rose sharply
  yesterday as investors positioned
  for an earlier-than-expected central
  bank rate cut. The FTSE 100 gained
  1.8%, while the S&P 500 added 2.1%.

  Bond markets also rallied, with the
  10-year gilt yield falling 12bps to
  3.94%. [CONTINUED COLUMN 2]

COLUMN 2:
  [CONTINUED FROM COLUMN 1]
  Economists at three major banks
  revised their rate cut forecasts
  forward by one meeting each.

  "The data is increasingly pointing
  to disinflation," said Maria Fontaine,
  Chief Economist at Meridian Capital.

  RELATED: Currency Markets p.7
  RELATED: Commodities Roundup p.9

COLUMN 3:
  OPINION
  ────────────────────
  RATE CUTS: TRIUMPH
  OR TRAP?
  By Prof. Alan Ridley

  Markets are celebrating, but history
  suggests premature easing carries
  risks. The 1998 episode taught us
  that cutting too early can re-ignite
  inflationary pressures.

  We should be cautious about reading
  too much into one data point.

COLUMN 4 (NARROW — SIDEBAR):
  AT A GLANCE
  ──────────────
  FTSE 100:  +1.8%
  S&P 500:   +2.1%
  EUR/USD:   1.0847
  Gold:      $2,198/oz
  Oil (Brent):$82.40/bbl
  10yr Gilt: 3.94%
